title: Within-lap ranking specialist for relative pit urgency
summary: At a given `Year/Race/LapNumber`, strategy is partly relative: some drivers are much closer to the pit window than others.
rationale: At a given `Year/Race/LapNumber`, strategy is partly relative: some drivers are much closer to the pit window than others. Ranking objectives can learn who is most pit-ready within the same lap even if absolute probabilities are noisy, and that relative ordering is often what helps ROC AUC.
implementation_hint: Train a LightGBM/CatBoost ranking model with groups=`Year,Race,LapNumber`, target=`PitNextLap`, using current numeric features plus field-relative deltas. Blend its out-of-fold ranks with the main classifier rather than replacing probabilities directly.
expected_effect: Improved ordering among same-lap competitors, which can add incremental AUC even when absolute calibration stays unchanged.
risk: Groups are small, positives are sparse, and ranking loss may ignore useful cross-lap information; needs careful blending.
